evals.prompt: I have Tesla_Research_CN.html in my report folder with financial_data.json next to it. Generate a complete Tesla_Research_CN.card_slots.json beside it, then tell me the exact validate_cards.py and generate_social_cards.py commands with --slots so I get all six PNGs.
evals.expected_output: Ask which color palette (macaron / default / b / c) before any work; then complete card_slots.json; both CLI commands with absolute paths and --brand; generate_social_cards.py must include --palette matching the customer choice; no export without --slots.

evals.prompt: Put the key takeaways from this equity research HTML into the fixed social card slots — same six-card layout as always — and make sure nothing clips before you export.
evals.expected_output: Follows two-agent pipeline (content then layout), full slot JSON, validation loop, then render; references design-spec budgets.

evals.prompt: Batch folder has three *_Research_CN.html files. Each needs its own card_slots.json. How do I pass --input and --slots to validate and render all three?
evals.expected_output: --input directory of HTMLs; --slots as directory containing matching <stem>.card_slots.json per file; no single JSON for unrelated stems.

evals.prompt: Export the six PNGs now — here is the path to my HTML and card_slots.json.
evals.expected_output: Do not run validate/generate until the customer has explicitly chosen macaron, default, b, or c; then pass --palette accordingly on generate_social_cards.py.
